content.verdict: Dedon Island Resort is the Philippines' most design-conscious luxury resort — a private island off Siargao's eastern coast where German luxury outdoor furniture brand DEDON has created seven casitas in a coconut grove, each furnished with the brand's signature woven rattan pieces and positioned around an infinity pool in a setting of almost architectural serenity. The castaway chic aesthetic, the surf access to Cloud 9, the yoga pavilion, and the exceptional kitchen make this one of Asia's most stylish and romantic small resorts.
content.best_room: All seven casitas are at a similar standard — spacious, beautifully furnished with DEDON pieces, and positioned around the central coconut grove. Request the casitas furthest from the main pavilion for maximum privacy, or those positioned facing the sunset over the channel between the island and Siargao. The pool itself is shared but the intimacy of only seven casitas means you frequently have it to yourselves.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private Island Arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: From Siargao's General Luna, a short outrigger boat (10 minutes) crosses to the island. The coconut grove, the DEDON furniture arranged artfully in open pavilions, and the infinity pool appearing through the trees establish the aesthetic tone immediately. This is not a conventional luxury resort — it is an artist's vision of an ideal castaway retreat. Dinner is a single shared table of guests: the kitchen's market-driven menu is always a surprise.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cloud 9 Surfing Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: The outrigger crosses back to Siargao for a morning at Cloud 9 — 20 minutes away by tricycle from the mainland landing point. The resort arranges beginner lessons with certified instructors on the easier reef breaks, while more experienced surfers can take on the famous hollow right-hander itself. Return to the island for a late lunch of freshly caught fish ceviche and cold coconut water.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Island Day & Naked Island Hopping
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend the morning on the resort island itself — the private beach, the pool, and the hammock under the coconut palms. After lunch, the resort boat visits Naked Island (the sandbar), Daku Island, and Guyam Island — the classic Siargao trilogy. The contrast between your exclusive private island home and these neighbouring public sandbars adds perspective to the luxury.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Yoga & Forager's Kitchen Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: DEDON Island's yoga programme runs on the open beach pavilion — the sound of Pacific surf and the coconut fronds create the setting. Morning yoga, a slow breakfast, then afternoon cooking with the kitchen team: the resort's menu philosophy is hyper-local and seasonal, using fishing village markets, garden herbs, and foraged coastal ingredients. Guests who join the kitchen get a deeper understanding of what makes the food exceptional.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sugba Lagoon Kayaking
itinerary_7_nights.description: The resort boat takes you to the Del Carmen mangrove area for access to Sugba Lagoon — an enormous emerald-green lagoon of extraordinary clarity, ringed by mangrove forest. Kayak the inner channels, swim from floating rafts, and experience a silence and natural beauty completely unlike the surf beaches. Return to DEDON for sunset drinks on the main pavilion terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa & Private Dinner on the Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: The spa uses traditional Filipino massage techniques and locally harvested plant oils — the resort's approach to wellness is understated and genuine rather than commercialised. Book a late morning couples' massage, then request the resort's private beach dinner arrangement for the evening: a table in the sand, the Pacific horizon, and the kitchen's best seasonal menu served by lantern light.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Your final morning on a private Philippine island: early swim in the infinity pool as the coconut palms catch the first light, followed by a breakfast of native Siargao produce on the pavilion. The outrigger crosses back to the Siargao mainland for the tricycle to the airport. DEDON Island is what all the overhyped 'private island' hotels are trying to be — and rarely are.

caveats: DEDON Island accommodates only seven casitas — if the resort is fully booked, the single shared pool and communal dining create a more social atmosphere than some honeymoon couples prefer. The resort's small scale is its greatest charm but also means you share the island's common spaces.
The resort was affected by Typhoon Odette in December 2021 — it has since been rebuilt, but confirm current operational status and condition directly with the resort before booking.

Access to Cloud 9 surfing requires a boat crossing and tricycle to the surf break — it is not immediately on-site. Non-surfers who don't plan to leave the island may find seven days of resort life limiting; combine with a night or two in General Luna for restaurant variety.

faqs.question: Is Dedon Island Resort good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Exceptionally so for design-conscious couples who value aesthetic coherence, excellent food, and intimate scale over conventional luxury trappings. The castaway chic atmosphere, private island format, and the DEDON-designed environment create a honeymoon that feels genuinely personal and artistic. The surfing and yoga options add active dimensions to a primarily contemplative experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: March to May for the calmest seas and most reliable weather on Siargao. September to November for the best surf at Cloud 9 — but this period carries typhoon risk. December to February is good with occasional rough patches. June to August is acceptable but can be variable. Avoid November to December typhoon peak in particularly active years.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: With only seven casitas, DEDON Island books out faster than any comparable resort in the Philippines — reserve 3 to 6 months ahead for peak season. The private beach dinner should be requested at booking. Island-hopping and Sugba Lagoon trips are arranged by the resort through their boat team — confirm availability on arrival.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: Not officially, but DEDON Island's price point and design philosophy mean it attracts almost exclusively adult guests — couples, design enthusiasts, and surf-culture cognoscenti. Very few families with young children choose this property. The shared pool and communal dinner format create a naturally adult atmosphere.

faqs.question: What's the best room for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Request the casitas in the most secluded position — furthest from the main pavilion, deepest in the coconut grove, or most directly facing the sunset channel. All seven casitas are at a similar quality level; position and privacy are the differentiators. The DEDON furniture and design are consistent throughout.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly from Manila or Cebu to Siargao's Sayak Airport (approximately 2 hours from Manila). From the airport, tricycle to General Luna takes 30 minutes. From the General Luna waterfront, DEDON Island's outrigger boat crosses to the private island in approximately 10 minutes. The resort coordinates transfers — contact them for the specific pickup arrangement.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Dedon Island Resort

Dear Dedon Island Resort team,

My partner and I are planning our honeymoon and are very interested in staying with you from [DATE] to [DATE] ([X] nights).

We are particularly interested in: one of your casitas — ideally the most private and sunset-facing available.

Could you please let us know:
- Current availability for our dates
- Any honeymoon packages or special arrangements
- Whether a private beach dinner can be organised during our stay

Thank you for your time.

[Your names]
